summary:
The most appropriate answer based on these considerations is A. Staphylococcus albus due to its non-invasive nature within human hosts; thus, it’s significantly less associated with conditions such as infective endocarditis when compared against options B through D which have documented cases involving this condition despite their lower frequency relative to some pathogens mentioned here too.
The final selected choice should reflect understanding that S.aureus often leads among staphylocci isolates linked directly towards IE yet still ranks higher than all else provided except perhaps one specific strain known rarely though uncommonly even amongst those already discussed herein.
